A stuck repository lock no longer breaks your integrity check, the "What's new" dialog retries instead of failing, and the whole web UI moves to the current front-end generation.

## Fixed
- **A stale repository lock no longer fails your integrity check (#92).** When a backup or check is interrupted (the container is stopped or restarted mid-run), restic can leave a lock behind that its own stale-detection misses inside a container: after a restart it can no longer verify the old process, and a lock under 30 minutes old is kept. The next **Verify** then failed with "repository is already locked" and needed a manual unlock. Verify now takes BombVault's own per-repo lock first, so it reports a clean "busy" if a backup is genuinely running; and if the lock is instead an orphan from a crashed run, it force-clears the lock and retries the check once, so the verify heals itself. A real integrity failure (damaged data) is still reported as-is; only lock conflicts auto-heal.
- **The "What's new" dialog now retries instead of showing "Couldn't load the release notes" (#68).** If the dialog opened in the brief moment the container was restarting to apply an update, its one release-notes request could land in that gap and fail once. It now auto-retries a few times with backoff and offers a manual "Try again" button if it still cannot load.

## Changed
- **Web UI modernized.** The interface was rebuilt on the current front-end generation (React 19, React Router 7, Vite 8, TypeScript 7 and Tailwind CSS 4), along with refreshed Go and JavaScript dependencies. There is no behaviour change; this keeps BombVault on supported, secure libraries.
